title: White House Launches AI-Driven ‘Gold Eagle’ Vulnerability Coordination Initiative
desc:
The new program stems from an AI-focused Executive Order signed by President Trump on June 2.

title: Progress Confirms Zero-Day Vulnerability Behind ShareFile Disruption
desc:
The company has rolled out a fix and is restoring access for Storage Zones Controller customers who apply it.

title: ICS Patch Tuesday: Vulnerabilities Fixed by Siemens, Schneider, Rockwell
desc: The industrial giants fixed dozens of vulnerabilities across their ICS products, with advisories also released by CISA and VDE CERT.

title: Critical Vulnerabilities Patched With Fresh Chrome 150, Firefox 152 Updates
desc:
Public exploit code targeting the Firefox flaws exists, but no in-the-wild exploitation has been observed.

title: SonicWall Issues Urgent SMA Patch Warning for Two Zero-Day Exploits
desc:
SonicWall SMA1000 zero-day vulnerabilities CVE-2026-15409 and CVE-2026-15410 can be exploited for remote code execution.

title: Microsoft Patches Record 622 Vulnerabilities, Including Two Exploited Zero-Days
desc: Two flaws in Active Directory and SharePoint Server have been exploited as zero-days, and a BitLocker bug was publicly disclosed.

title: Synopsys Finds No Evidence of Data Breach Amid Bosch Hack Claims
desc: The D1R cybercrime group claimed to have stolen valuable data from Synopsys and Bosch, threatening to leak it unless a ransom is paid. </

title: Adobe Patches Critical ColdFusion Vulnerabilities
desc:
The ColdFusion security defects could allow attackers to execute arbitrary code or elevate their privileges.
